summary: Deterministic proof that `discover claims` helper flags cover prior-state refresh, carry-forward, from-scratch opt-out, and refresh-plan packet ownership without introducing a separate refresh command.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: discover claims refresh helper flags and carry-forward state
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the contract claims about `discover claims --previous`, helper flags, carried-forward reviewed state, and deterministic refresh-plan ownership are marked satisfied.
notClaimed: A refresh-plan packet is itself the saved claim map update.
LLM-backed review or subagent orchestration is performed by the binary.
Every carried evidence ref remains trusted without stale-evidence rechecks.
A separate public `claim refresh` command exists.
Host agents can skip the Cautilus Agent refresh instructions.

protectedBehaviors: unchanged claim fingerprints carry forward reviewed labels, evidence refs, unresolved questions, and next-action state
carried evidence refs rewrite supportsClaimIds when display claim ids drift
changed carried evidence becomes stale instead of remaining blindly satisfied
refresh-plan packets record changed sources, changed claims, carried-forward counts, and stale evidence reasons
command registry exposes discover claims helper flags
existing output claim packets are auto-used as previous state unless from-scratch is requested
refresh-plan output refuses to overwrite saved claim-state proof-plan paths

decision.reason: Skill parity keeps the Cautilus Agent refresh branch documented, runtime tests prove carry-forward and stale-evidence transitions, and app tests plus command registry prove `discover claims` helper flags cover refresh planning without a separate refresh command.
